nodes.name: Parse workbook
nodes.documentation: System parses the uploaded file into rows. Row numbering convention: header row = 1, first data row = 2 (used in every error message so the admin can find the cell). No data is written during parse. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA parse + row-number convention]

nodes.name: Validate every row + resolve refs, ACCUMULATE errors
nodes.documentation: Iterate every data row (NEVER fail-fast). Per row: type/format/required-field checks against the masterType schema, then resolve foreign references (e.g. a supplier's group, an item's UoM/segment) via the entity normalizer. Each failure is appended to an error list as {row, header/field, reason} using the header=1/first-data=2 numbering. The loop continues to the last row so the admin sees ALL problems in one pass, not just the first. No write occurs in this step. [SCOR OE4 | ISO 7.5 | source: RA accumulate-all-errors, never fail-fast]

nodes.name: Return one BadRequest listing all {row, reason}; write nothing
nodes.documentation: Single 400 response with the full error list (every failing row and reason). No partial import: zero rows are written. The admin fixes the file and re-uploads. Audit logs the rejected import attempt with the error count. [SCOR OE4 | ISO 7.5 records | source: RA reject-all]

nodes.name: Single transaction: upsert by natural key
nodes.documentation: Open ONE database transaction. For each row, upsert by the master's NATURAL KEY (e.g. supplier code S/#####, item code DEP/SG/SSG/######, UoM code, currency code, tax code): existing key -> UPDATE (count as updated), new key -> INSERT (count as created; imported records default PENDING_ONBOARDING for supplier/item). All rows share the one transaction. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA natural-key upsert, single transaction]

nodes.name: Rollback all; report failure
nodes.documentation: Roll the single transaction back so no rows persist, and report the failure. The pre-write validation already caught content errors; a rollback here is for an integrity/DB error during write. Audit logs the failed transaction. [SCOR OE4 | ISO 7.5 | source: RA rollback-all]

nodes.name: Commit, audit, emit SSE
nodes.documentation: On commit: write an append-only audit entry (category admin) recording masterType, created count, updated count, and the actor; emit SSE so master lists refresh. Imported supplier/item rows that were inserted are PENDING_ONBOARDING and enter the onboarding queue (06/07). [SCOR OE4 | ISO 7.5 records | source: platform-services audit + RA]

nodes.name: Return {created, updated}
nodes.documentation: Respond with the summary {created: n, updated: m}. This is the success contract of the pipeline; combined with the all-or-nothing rule it guarantees the import either fully applied or not at all. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA result contract]

nodes.name: File readable?
nodes.documentation: Expression: parser opened the file AND format in {XLSX, CSV} AND not corrupt/empty-bytes. False -> BadRequest 'file not readable / unsupported format', abort (nothing written). True -> check the sheet. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA pipeline guard 1]

nodes.name: Expected sheet present?
nodes.documentation: Expression: the workbook contains the expected sheet for masterType (XLSX) or the CSV has a content block. False -> BadRequest 'expected sheet missing', abort. True -> check for data rows. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA pipeline guard 2]

nodes.name: At least one data row?
nodes.documentation: Expression: rowCount > 1 (more than the header row). False -> BadRequest 'no data rows', abort. True -> validate headers. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA pipeline guard 3]

nodes.name: Headers exactly match schema?
nodes.documentation: Exact header validation. Expression: set(actualHeaders) == set(expectedHeaders for masterType): NO missing columns AND NO extra columns AND no duplicates. A mismatch is a STRUCTURAL failure reported before any row work. False -> BadRequest listing the missing and the extra headers, abort. True -> per-row validation. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA exact-header validation, no missing/extra]

nodes.name: Any row errors accumulated?
nodes.documentation: Expression: errorList.length > 0. True -> emit ONE BadRequest whose body lists every {row, reason}; NOTHING is written (the whole file is rejected together). False -> proceed to the single-transaction upsert. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA one-error-response, all-or-nothing]

nodes.name: All rows persisted without error?
nodes.documentation: Expression: every upsert in the transaction succeeded (no DB constraint violation, no late integrity error). True -> COMMIT all. False -> ROLLBACK all (commit-all-or-rollback-all); return a 500/409 with the failure; nothing persists. There is no partial commit. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA commit-or-rollback-all]

nodes.name: Upload XLSX / CSV for a master
nodes.documentation: Trigger: an administrator uploads a workbook (XLSX or CSV) for one master entity. One generic pipeline serves every master: suppliers, items, UoM, currency, segments, payment terms, warehouses, asset proposals, supplier groups, tax codes. Field masterType (dropdown, mandatory) selects the schema and the natural key. File picked via the import action (permission *.import, e.g. suppliers.import / items.import; Admin always). [SCOR OE4 | source: RA bulk import e06]

nodes.name: Import committed
nodes.documentation: Master data updated atomically; counts returned; audit + SSE emitted. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA]

nodes.name: Import rejected; nothing written
nodes.documentation: Terminal reject path for a structural failure (unreadable / no sheet / no rows / header mismatch) or accumulated row errors. The whole file is rejected; the admin corrects and re-uploads. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA all-or-nothing]
